articles.title: Government Plans Law Change to Deport Rochdale Grooming Gang Leader Shabir Ahmed
articles.excerpt: There are renewed calls for legal changes to facilitate the deportation of a Rochdale rapist leader, highlighting ongoing concerns about justice and immigration policy.
articles.content_html:
A renewed call for changes to immigration law has emerged following the case involving a Rochdale rapist leader. The discussion has gained traction after statements made by the Home Secretary, who has faced scrutiny regarding her approach to the issue.

The Home Secretary, of Pakistani heritage, has previously stated her commitment to prioritising her faith. Critics have questioned her ability to balance this with her responsibilities in addressing serious criminal cases, particularly those involving sexual offences.

The case of the Rochdale rapist leader has prompted concerns among community members and activists. Many believe that the legal framework should be adjusted to ensure that individuals convicted of serious crimes can be deported more efficiently.

Historically, there have been calls for reform in this area, with advocates arguing that the current laws are insufficient to deal with the complexities of immigration and criminal justice. The lack of action on this front has led to frustration among those affected by such crimes.

As discussions continue, it remains unclear what specific changes may be proposed or implemented. The situation highlights the intersection of immigration policy and public safety, a topic that is likely to remain in the spotlight as the government navigates these challenges.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article. Our thoughts are with the family and all those affected.

articles.title: Man Arrested Following Death of Woman in Milnrow Farm Fire
articles.excerpt: A man has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a woman was found dead in a fire at a farm in Milnrow. The incident occurred on July 3 at Higher Tunshill Farm.
articles.content_html:
A man has been arrested on suspicion of murder following the death of a woman in a fire at a farm in Milnrow. The incident took place on Friday, July 3, at Higher Tunshill Farm, located off Tunshill Lane.

Emergency services, including police, firefighters and paramedics, were called to the scene at around 12.30pm. Upon arrival, they discovered the body of a woman inside the stables. The victim has since been named as Laura Foulis, aged in her 30s.

Following the incident, a man in his 40s was taken into custody by Greater Manchester Police. He has since been bailed pending further investigation. A spokesperson for Rochdale Coroners' Court confirmed that a referral has been made regarding Ms Foulis' death, and an inquest will be opened at a later date.

Witnesses reported seeing large flames and thick black smoke rising from the stables, prompting a swift response from emergency services. Forensic investigators remained at the scene for several days following the fire, which has since been cordoned off as part of the ongoing investigation.

Tributes have been shared on social media, with friends and family remembering Ms Foulis as a kind and beautiful person who inspired many with her love of horses. One tribute described her as a 'kind soul' who made a significant impact on those around her.

The investigation into the circumstances surrounding the fire is ongoing, and police have maintained a scene at the farm as they gather evidence. Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Temporary fire station in Heywood approved during redevelopment
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service has secured approval for a temporary fire station in Heywood, ensuring continuous emergency services while the existing facility undergoes significant redevelopment.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — This report brings together confirmed developments from multiple sources.

Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) has received approval to establish a temporary fire station in Heywood, facilitating the redevelopment of the existing facility. The new structure will be situated behind the current station on Middleton Road, ensuring that emergency response services remain operational throughout the rebuilding process.

The existing fire station, which has served the Heywood community for nearly 60 years, is scheduled for demolition as part of this redevelopment initiative. The temporary station will consist of a two-storey modular building, equipped with a bay for one fire engine, while an additional outbuilding will house any specialist vehicles. This arrangement is intended to guarantee that emergency services continue without interruption during the construction period.

Planning documents reveal that the temporary fire station is strategically located just 400 metres from Junction 19 of the M62 motorway, positioning it well for rapid emergency response. GMFRS has stated that the new facility will be a modern, fit-for-purpose community fire station, designed to meet both current and future operational needs. It will also enhance facilities for staff training and community engagement, reflecting the service's commitment to adapting to the evolving demands of the community.

This redevelopment is part of a wider £38 million Estates Strategy by GMFRS, aimed at upgrading and modernising several fire stations across the Greater Manchester area. The strategy underscores the importance of ensuring that fire services can provide optimal support to residents, adapting to changing community needs and operational requirements. A separate planning application for the demolition of the existing station and the construction of the new community fire station is expected to be submitted shortly.

The anticipated new facility will include a training tower, vehicle parking, and a dedicated training yard, which will further enhance GMFRS's capabilities in the region. For local families, businesses, and schools, the uninterrupted provision of emergency services during the redevelopment is crucial, as it ensures that safety and response times remain unaffected. Local residents and stakeholders are encouraged to monitor the progress of these plans, as the redevelopment will play a vital role in maintaining safety and emergency response in Heywood. Updates will be disseminated through GMFRS and local news outlets as the project advances.

articles.title: Service Changes on Rochdale Line Due to Planned Works
articles.excerpt: Planned improvement works on the Rochdale line will affect services until late August, with additional lift outages at Central Park and Queens Road tram stops.
articles.content_html:
Planned works on the Rochdale line will result in service changes from Monday, 18 May, until Wednesday, 19 August. Essential improvement works are set to take place at the Prestwich Metrolink stop. Although tram services will not be directly affected, passengers may notice an increased presence of staff at this location during the improvement period.

In addition to the works at Prestwich, there are ongoing lift outages at two tram stops. As of Friday, 10 July, the lift at Central Park, which serves the platform towards Rochdale via Oldham, is out of service. Customers travelling towards Oldham can access the Manchester-bound platform via lift from The Gateway and then use the track crossing to reach the Oldham-bound platform.

Similarly, the lift at Queens Road tram stop is also out of service as of Friday, 10 July. Passengers requiring step-free access are advised to contact the Metrolink contact centre for assistance with journey planning. The contact centre can be reached at 0161 244 1000, with lines open from 7am to 8pm on weekdays and 8am to 8pm on weekends and Bank Holidays.

Passengers are encouraged to plan their journeys accordingly, as these changes may affect travel times and accessibility. It is advisable to check the latest updates on the Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) travel alerts page for any further developments regarding service changes and lift availability.

The planned works at Prestwich are part of ongoing improvements to enhance the Metrolink network, ensuring better service reliability for passengers. TfGM continues to monitor the situation and will provide updates as necessary.

For more information on travel disruptions and updates, visit the TfGM travel alerts page at TfGM Travel Alerts.

articles.title: Reform mayoral candidate Sian Astley stands by controversial migrant comments
articles.excerpt: Reform UK’s Greater Manchester mayoral candidate Sian Astley has defended comments suggesting Green voters and politicians should house people she described as illegal criminals, rapists and economic chancers.
articles.summary: Sian Astley said her comments were partly tongue-in-cheek and directed at supporters of open-border policies, while Labour accused Reform UK of seeking to punish communities according to how they vote.
articles.content_html:
Reform UK’s candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester has said she stands by controversial comments suggesting supporters of other political parties should take responsibility for housing people she described as illegal criminals, rapists and economic chancers.

Sian Astley made the comments while sharing a Reform UK proposal concerning the location of migrant detention centres.

Under the proposal, facilities would be prioritised in areas represented or controlled by Green Party politicians, while areas with Reform UK-controlled councils or MPs would not receive them.

Astley suggested that Green voters, politicians from what she called the “uniparty” and councillors should instead house the people concerned themselves.

Speaking to the Manchester Evening News after the comments attracted criticism, Astley said she stood by what she had written.

She described the suggestion as partly “tongue in cheek” and argued that people who advocate open-border policies should accept the consequences of those policies.

Labour criticism

Labour Party chair Anna Turley criticised both the comments and the wider proposal.

She accused Reform UK of supporting a policy that would reward or punish communities according to how residents voted and said this undermined basic democratic principles.

Astley has not withdrawn the comments and said criticism should instead be directed at the immigration system and those entering the country unlawfully.

Greater Manchester mayoral election

Astley is Reform UK’s official candidate in the Greater Manchester mayoral election taking place on Thursday 30 July 2026.

Residents across Rochdale borough and the rest of Greater Manchester will be eligible to vote for the next mayor, with polling stations open from 7am until 10pm.

Astley is also a Manchester city councillor and leads the Reform UK group on Manchester City Council.

The original Facebook post was not supplied directly to Rochdale Daily. The comments and Astley’s subsequent defence have been reported by the Manchester Evening News and The Guardian.

Manchester Evening News source

articles.title: Health experts issue guidance on safe fan use amid Rochdale heatwave
articles.excerpt: As temperatures rise, health experts in Rochdale provide essential advice on the safe operation of electric fans to mitigate overheating and electrical hazards.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — This report brings together confirmed developments from multiple sources.

As the UK endures a significant heatwave, health and safety experts are advising Rochdale residents on the safe operation of electric fans. This guidance, issued by High Speed Training, underscores the importance of taking precautions to prevent overheating and potential electrical hazards as households increasingly rely on fans for relief from the soaring temperatures.

Dr Richard Anderson, Director of Learning at High Speed Training, emphasised the effectiveness of fans in providing comfort during hot weather while also stressing the necessity of their proper use to ensure safety. He noted that as temperatures rise, many individuals will be reaching for their fans, but adherence to safety protocols is crucial to avoid accidents.

Residents should register new fans with their manufacturers to receive important updates regarding safety recalls. Before using a fan, it is vital to inspect it for any signs of damage, particularly focusing on the plug and cable. Fans that produce buzzing or crackling noises, or exhibit warm plugs, should be turned off immediately and examined by a qualified electrician. Regular cleaning is also important; dust accumulation on fan blades can strain the motor and increase the risk of overheating. Users must always unplug the fan before cleaning and follow the manufacturer's reassembly instructions.

Furthermore, it is advisable to avoid leaving fans running unattended or overnight while sleeping. Instead, residents might consider cooling their bedrooms prior to bedtime by operating the fan with windows open when appropriate. Proper positioning of fans is equally important; they should be placed on a flat, stable surface and kept clear of curtains or other materials that could obstruct airflow or create hazards.

Dr Anderson reiterated the significance of these safety measures, asserting that dedicating a few moments to simple safety checks can prevent fires or accidents and ensure fans operate smoothly. With the heatwave projected to persist for at least another week, adhering to these safety tips will be crucial for maintaining comfort and safety during this period. For further information or to report safety concerns, residents can contact local health and safety authorities.

articles.title: Trial begins for man accused of murdering teenager in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King is on trial for the alleged murder of 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who was struck by King's car after stealing an e-bike.
articles.content_html:
A trial has commenced at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court for Terrence King, who faces allegations of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines in Middleton last year. The incident occurred on 22 July 2025, when Simmonds-Caines was struck by King's vehicle after allegedly stealing an e-bike from another boy.

The prosecution, led by Michael Hayton KC, contends that King, 60, deliberately drove his car at Simmonds-Caines as an act of revenge. CCTV and doorbell camera footage presented in court reportedly shows King accelerating before veering onto the pavement and colliding with the teenager, who succumbed to his injuries at the scene.

King has admitted to manslaughter, acknowledging that his actions resulted in Simmonds-Caines's death, but he denies the charge of murder. The prosecution argues that this incident was not merely a tragic accident or a case of poor driving, but rather a calculated act of aggression against a boy who had stolen the e-bike.

The footage shown in court was described as distressing, prompting some family members of Simmonds-Caines to leave the courtroom in tears. This case has attracted significant attention, particularly due to the victim's age and the circumstances surrounding his death, raising broader concerns about youth crime and vigilantism.

The community in Middleton is now grappling with the implications of such incidents, which raise pressing questions about safety and the appropriate responses to crime. The trial is expected to continue over the coming weeks, with further evidence and testimonies to be presented. Local residents, particularly those affected by the incident, may be closely following the proceedings as they seek to comprehend the full context of this tragic event and its impact on community safety.

articles.excerpt: Rochdale Council's new traffic scheme at Hopwood Triangle aims to enhance road safety, but local residents have expressed mixed feelings about its potential impact on congestion and travel times.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Council has launched a trial traffic scheme at the Hopwood Triangle in Heywood, aimed at improving road safety in the area. This initiative has elicited a range of responses from local residents, with some welcoming the changes while others voice concerns about possible disruptions to traffic flow.

The scheme was introduced following extensive consultations with the community, during which residents highlighted issues related to traffic management and safety. The council's objective is to address persistent concerns regarding speeding vehicles and pedestrian safety, particularly for vulnerable groups such as children and the elderly.

Since the implementation of the scheme, feedback from residents has been varied. Proponents argue that the changes are essential for reducing accidents and enhancing safety for all road users. They contend that the new measures will promote more responsible driving behaviours and create a safer environment for pedestrians.

Conversely, some residents have expressed apprehension regarding the scheme's effectiveness. Critics suggest that the alterations may lead to increased congestion and longer travel times, particularly during peak hours. There are fears that the new traffic patterns could confuse drivers who are unfamiliar with the area, potentially negating the intended safety benefits.

The council has committed to closely monitoring the trial, with plans to gather feedback from residents to evaluate the scheme's impact. A review is scheduled to take place at the end of the trial period, which will determine whether the changes will be made permanent or adjusted based on community input. Local residents can submit their views through the council's official channels, ensuring that all perspectives are considered in this important safety initiative.

The implications of this trial scheme extend beyond mere traffic management. Families, particularly those with children, may find the changes reassuring if they lead to improved safety. However, businesses in the area could experience fluctuations in customer traffic if congestion increases, potentially affecting their operations. Schools nearby may also need to adapt to any changes in travel patterns, as parents and guardians navigate the new traffic flow. Overall, the outcome of this trial could significantly influence daily life in the Hopwood Triangle, making community feedback a crucial element in shaping the future of this initiative.

articles.title: Whitworth councillor challenges council over play park funding gap
articles.excerpt: Whitworth councillor Scott Smith has challenged Rossendale Borough Council over play park funding, claiming the town is receiving no comparable investment while substantial sums are being spent elsewhere.
articles.summary: Rossendale opposition leader Scott Smith says council officers will meet Whitworth Town Council within two months after he questioned what he described as unequal play park investment.
articles.content_html:
A Whitworth councillor has questioned why the town is not receiving what he considers the same level of play park investment as other parts of Rossendale.

Councillor Scott Smith, leader of the opposition at Rossendale Borough Council, raised the issue during question time connected to the full council meeting held on Wednesday 8 July.

“What on Earth will it take for Whitworth to receive equal treatment to every other town in this borough?”

In a social-media statement, Councillor Smith claimed that £140,000 is being spent on Edgeside Park and a further £143,000 on Whitaker Park in Rawtenstall during 2026, while Whitworth is currently set to receive no comparable play park investment.

He said a cabinet member committed council officers to meeting representatives of Whitworth Town Council within two months to put together a plan aimed at changing the position.

Councillor Smith said he intended to hold the council administration to that commitment.

Edgeside investment confirmed

Rossendale Borough Council announced on 7 July that it was moving ahead with the next phase of improvements to the top play area at Edgeside Park.

The council said the project was being supported through Pride in Place funding, with additional investment secured through the Derbyshire Environmental Fund via the Landfill Communities Fund.

However, the council's announcement did not state the £140,000 figure cited by Councillor Smith. Rochdale Daily has also not independently confirmed the £143,000 Whitaker Park figure or the claim that no comparable play park funding has been allocated to Whitworth.

Right of reply

Rossendale Borough Council is welcome to provide clarification about the funding figures and the proposed meeting with Whitworth Town Council.

Source
articles.legal_disclaimer: The spending figures and account of the proposed meeting are attributed to Councillor Scott Smith. Rossendale Borough Council's published Edgeside Park announcement confirms investment but does not state the figures cited.

articles.title: Students Evacuated from Wardle Academy After Handheld Fan Overheats
articles.excerpt: Students at Wardle Academy were evacuated on Thursday after a handheld fan overheated, prompting a response from the fire service. No injuries were reported and lessons resumed shortly after the incident.
articles.content_html:
A small handheld fan overheated at Wardle Academy on Thursday, leading to the temporary evacuation of students from the school. The incident prompted the school to seek advice from Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service, which attended the scene to assess the situation.

In a message sent to parents and carers, the school explained that the evacuation was a precautionary measure while the overheating fan was assessed. Firefighters confirmed that the building was safe for students and staff to return shortly after their arrival.

Initial reports on social media suggested that the fan had exploded; however, the school clarified that the incident involved an overheating device rather than an explosion. No injuries were reported as a result of the incident.

Teaching resumed soon after the fire service confirmed the safety of the building. The school expressed satisfaction with how students responded during the evacuation, stating that all learners remained calm throughout the process.

Wardle Academy has reassured parents and guardians that safety is a priority and that appropriate measures were taken to ensure the well-being of students during the incident. The school is committed to maintaining a safe learning environment for all pupils.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article. Our thoughts are with the family and all those affected.

articles.title: Kirkholt Pantry marks decade of vital community support
articles.excerpt: The Kirkholt Pantry has celebrated its 10th anniversary, recognising a decade of essential food assistance to local families amid ongoing economic challenges.
articles.content_html:
The Kirkholt Pantry recently commemorated its 10th anniversary with a special event at The Strand, celebrating a decade of unwavering support for families in the Kirkholt area. This community initiative has been instrumental in providing essential food assistance, particularly during a period of rising living costs that has placed significant financial strain on many households.

Since its establishment, the pantry has distributed over 23,000 bags of essential food items, offering affordable access to groceries for those in need. Each week, members can collect a bag of vital food supplies for just £4, making it an indispensable resource for numerous families facing economic hardship. The anniversary event brought together staff, patrons, and volunteers, including some of the original founders of the pantry, highlighting the collaborative spirit that has sustained this initiative.

Councillor Daniel Meredith, a key supporter of the pantry through donations and funding from Rochdale South Township, expressed his pride in the initiative. He referred to the pantry as a "lifeline for local residents," underscoring its critical role in alleviating financial pressures on families. In a message shared on social media, Councillor Meredith extended his gratitude to the volunteers and donors who have contributed to the pantry's success over the years, describing the project as a "shining example of community spirit and partnership in action."

The Kirkholt Pantry operates every Thursday from 10am to 12pm, continuing to serve as a vital resource for families grappling with economic difficulties. As the cost of living continues to escalate, the pantry's role in helping households manage their budgets and access essential food items is likely to become even more significant. Families relying on this service may find it increasingly important as they navigate the challenges posed by rising prices.

Looking to the future, the Kirkholt Pantry aims to expand its services to reach even more families in need. Community members are encouraged to support the pantry through donations or by volunteering, ensuring that this essential service can continue to thrive and adapt to the evolving needs of the local population. The ongoing commitment to supporting families in Kirkholt reflects a broader community effort to address food insecurity and foster resilience in the face of economic challenges.

As the situation develops, it remains essential for residents to remain aware of local drainage services and to maintain open lines of communication with both service providers and local authorities. articles.title: Castleton Library to host Bookstart events for young children
articles.excerpt: On Monday, 13 July, Castleton Library will host two Bookstart events aimed at promoting early literacy among young children, providing opportunities for parents to engage with their infants and toddlers.
articles.content_html:
Castleton Library will host two events on Monday, 13 July, aimed at fostering early literacy among young children. The first, the Bookstart Bear Club, is scheduled from 9:45 AM to 10:30 AM, followed by the Bookstart Baby session from 11 AM to 11:30 AM. Both events are designed to cultivate a love for reading in infants and toddlers, encouraging engagement between parents and their children.

The Bookstart Bear Club caters to children aged 12 months and older, offering an interactive environment filled with stories, songs, and rhymes. This session aims to encourage parents and caregivers to actively participate in reading activities, thereby instilling a positive attitude towards books from an early age. Following this, the Bookstart Baby event is tailored for infants up to 12 months old, providing a nurturing space for parents to bond with their babies while introducing them to the joys of literature through stories and songs.

Both events are free of charge, but advance booking is essential due to limited capacity. These initiatives are part of the national Bookstart programme, which seeks to provide free books to children and promote reading as a crucial component of early childhood development. The sessions at Castleton Library not only support literacy but also create opportunities for parents to connect with other families in the community, fostering a sense of belonging and shared experience.

For local families, participation in these events may enhance their children's early learning experiences significantly. Engaging with literature at a young age is linked to improved language skills and cognitive development, which could have lasting benefits as children progress through their education. Moreover, the social aspect of these sessions may help parents build networks of support within the community, which can be invaluable as they navigate the challenges of early parenthood.

Interested attendees can secure their places by booking tickets through Eventbrite, where additional details about the events are also available. As the date approaches, parents are encouraged to arrive a few minutes early to ensure a smooth start to the sessions. For further inquiries, Castleton Library can be contacted directly.

articles.title: Oulder Hill pupils discuss democracy during visit to Rochdale Town Hall
articles.excerpt: Pupils from Oulder Hill Leadership Academy met the Mayor and Mayoress of Rochdale during an educational visit to the Mayor’s Parlour and council chamber.
articles.summary: Mayor Councillor Faisal Rana and Mayoress Iram Faisal welcomed Oulder Hill pupils to Rochdale Town Hall, where students asked questions about civic leadership, local democracy and how the council serves the community.
articles.content_html:
Pupils from Oulder Hill Leadership Academy have visited Rochdale Town Hall to learn more about civic leadership, local democracy and the work of the borough council.

The students were welcomed to the Mayor’s Parlour and council chamber by the Mayor of Rochdale, Councillor Faisal Rana, and Mayoress Iram Faisal.

During the visit, pupils were given an opportunity to see some of the town hall’s civic rooms and sit inside the chamber where councillors debate and make decisions affecting communities across the borough.

The students asked questions about the role of the mayor, how local democracy works and how Rochdale Borough Council serves local residents.

They also spoke about their own ambitions and aspirations for the future.

Councillor Rana described it as an “absolute pleasure” to welcome the pupils and praised their confidence, curiosity and enthusiasm.

A personal connection to Oulder Hill

The mayor said the visit held particular significance for him because his own children attended Oulder Hill, with one having served as the school’s head boy.

He said seeing a new generation of pupils sitting in the chamber where important decisions are made for the borough was a special moment.

Councillor Rana congratulated the pupils for representing their school and thanked staff for helping to make the visit a success.

He said the young people should be proud of themselves and expressed his hope that they would go on to achieve their ambitions in the years ahead.

Learning about local government

Educational visits to civic buildings can give pupils a closer look at how local decisions are made and how elected representatives, council officers and ceremonial office holders serve their communities.

The visit also allowed the Oulder Hill pupils to raise their own questions directly with the borough’s mayor and mayoress.

This report is based on a public social-media statement and photographs shared by Mayor of Rochdale Councillor Faisal Rana. No pupils have been identified by name.

articles.title: Proposal for new SEND school at Kingsway West Business Park
articles.excerpt: Whitehall Academy has submitted plans to convert a vacant warehouse into a school for children with special educational needs and disabilities, accommodating up to 50 pupils.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — This report brings together confirmed developments from multiple sources.

A proposal has been submitted to transform a vacant warehouse at Kingsway West Business Park in Rochdale into a new educational facility for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND). The plans, put forward by Whitehall Academy, aim to create a school that will accommodate up to 50 pupils, providing essential support for children with complex needs.

The design of the proposed school includes six classrooms, three sensory rooms, and a multi-purpose hall, alongside various support spaces such as offices and a medical room. While the exterior of the building will remain unchanged, a secure play area featuring artificial grass is planned for the west side, utilising previously unused space. This initiative represents an extension of Whitehall Academy's existing operations on Whitehall Street, where the independent primary school already focuses on supporting vulnerable children with diverse needs.

Students at the proposed facility may include those diagnosed with autism and learning difficulties, all of whom will have an Education, Health, and Care Plan (EHCP) or be undergoing the assessment process. The school is expected to operate from 9am to 3pm for pupils, with staff hours extending from 8am to 4pm. There are also plans for breakfast and after-school clubs, which could extend the school day until 5pm, providing additional support for families.

The warehouse, previously occupied by Circuitmaster Designs Ltd, an engineering firm, has been vacant since October 2024. The conversion of this space into a SEND school is likely to address a pressing need for specialised educational facilities in the area, particularly as the demand for such services continues to grow.

The establishment of a SEND school at Kingsway West Business Park could have significant implications for local families and the wider community. Parents of children with special educational needs may find increased access to tailored educational resources and support, reducing the need for travel to distant facilities. Additionally, the school could foster a more inclusive environment within the neighbourhood, promoting awareness and understanding of SEND issues. As the proposal progresses, local stakeholders, including parents and community organisations, may wish to engage with the planning process to ensure that the needs of all affected parties are considered.

articles.title: New Community Zumba classes to launch in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Your Trust introduces Community Zumba classes in Rochdale, promoting health and fitness while fostering social connections among residents. Classes will cater to all fitness levels and will be held at various locations across the town.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale will soon see the introduction of Community Zumba classes, an initiative aimed at enhancing the health and fitness of local residents. Organised by Your Trust, these classes are designed to provide an enjoyable and engaging way for individuals to remain active while simultaneously fostering social connections within the community.

The Community Zumba classes will be held regularly, offering participants the chance to partake in energetic dance workouts led by experienced instructors. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to promote physical activity and well-being among residents of all ages, encouraging them to engage in regular exercise within a supportive environment. Zumba, a dance-based fitness programme, combines Latin and international music with dynamic dance moves, making it an appealing method for individuals to improve their fitness levels.

Your Trust has played a pivotal role in promoting health and fitness initiatives throughout Rochdale, and the introduction of these classes is expected to be a significant enhancement to their existing offerings. By creating a space for community members to gather, the programme aims to improve both physical health and mental well-being, which is particularly relevant in the context of ongoing public health discussions.

Classes will take place at various locations across Rochdale, with specific venues and times to be announced shortly. Residents interested in participating are encouraged to monitor local announcements for further details on registration and class schedules. This initiative not only promotes fitness but also seeks to strengthen community ties, making it a noteworthy development for the residents of Rochdale.

As the classes commence, local families, businesses, and schools may find opportunities to engage with this initiative. Increased participation in physical activities could lead to a healthier community overall, which may, in turn, benefit local services and businesses that cater to health and wellness. The potential for enhanced social interaction through these classes could also contribute positively to neighbourhood life, fostering a sense of belonging among participants.

articles.title: New Safe Haven offers support during nights out in Rochdale town centre
articles.excerpt: A new Safe Haven has been introduced in Rochdale town centre, offering practical help and a place to pause for residents and visitors during nights out.
articles.summary: The Rochdale Safe Haven provides phone charging, directions, water, first aid, emotional support and help getting home safely, with extended opening planned around major events and World Cup matches.
articles.content_html:
A new Safe Haven has been introduced in Rochdale town centre to provide practical help and reassurance to residents and visitors enjoying the town’s nightlife.

The mobile facility is positioned in Town Hall Square, beside the Gracie Fields statue, and provides a recognised place for anyone who experiences difficulty or feels vulnerable during a night out.

Visitors can use the Safe Haven to charge their phones, ask for directions, get some water or take a moment away from the busy night-time environment.

Support available can also include basic first aid, emotional support, help reporting an incident, assistance arranging a taxi and signposting to other services.

Saturday-night support

Council plans state that the Safe Haven will ordinarily operate every Saturday evening between 10pm and 3am.

The facility can also act as a trusted central point for licensed venues, street pastors, Greater Manchester Police and other organisations working to keep people safe in the town centre.

Rochdale Borough Council said residents may see more of the Safe Haven during the summer as the town hosts major events.

Extended opening hours are also planned around World Cup matches, when more people may be visiting pubs, bars and venues across the town centre.

Initial six-month trial

Funding from the Greater Manchester Combined Authority was secured to establish the service as part of wider work to improve safety in Rochdale’s night-time economy.

The initial scheme is expected to run for six months while its cost, demand and impact are reviewed.

The council has said the service could later be used more widely at events and potentially extended to other town centres across the borough.

This report is based on information published by Rochdale Borough Council and an official council committee report.

articles.title: Taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport: a comprehensive guide
articles.excerpt: A detailed overview of taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport, covering routes, fares, and booking options for local travellers.
articles.content_html:
Residents of Rochdale planning to travel to Manchester Airport can now refer to a detailed guide outlining the available taxi services for this route. The guide encompasses various routes, estimated fares, and booking options, enabling travellers to make informed decisions regarding their transportation.

The most frequently used taxi routes from Rochdale to Manchester Airport typically take between 30 to 45 minutes, contingent on traffic conditions. The A627(M) and M62 are the primary roads utilised, offering direct access to the airport. Travellers should consider checking real-time traffic updates to mitigate potential delays, particularly during peak travel periods.

Fares for taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport can fluctuate considerably based on the time of day and the specific taxi company selected. On average, passengers can anticipate costs ranging from £30 to £50 for a standard taxi. It is prudent to compare prices from various providers and consider pre-booking to secure the most favourable rates.

In addition to traditional taxi services, ride-sharing options are available for those seeking a more flexible approach. These services can be booked through mobile applications, allowing users to track their ride and manage payment options directly via their smartphones. This flexibility may appeal to families and individuals with varying schedules, enhancing convenience for all travellers.

As travel patterns continue to evolve, it is vital for Rochdale residents to remain aware of the best transportation options at their disposal. Reliable taxi services can significantly improve the travel experience, whether for business or leisure. For further information or to book a taxi, residents can visit local taxi company websites or contact them directly for assistance. This accessibility ensures that families, commuters, and businesses can plan their journeys effectively, minimising stress and maximising efficiency.

articles.title: New Initiative to Teach Social and Emotional Learning in Early Years
articles.excerpt: A new programme called Think Equal aims to enhance social and emotional learning for children in early years education across Rochdale.
articles.content_html:
A new programme called Think Equal is set to enhance social and emotional learning for children in early years education across Rochdale. The initiative focuses on equipping young learners with essential skills to understand themselves and others, fostering empathy and resilience from a young age.

Think Equal aims to integrate social and emotional learning into the curriculum, providing educators with resources and training to effectively teach these vital skills. The programme is designed to support children's emotional development, helping them navigate their feelings and build positive relationships with peers.

The initiative has been welcomed by local educators, who recognise the importance of social and emotional learning in early childhood. By introducing these concepts early, the programme seeks to lay a strong foundation for children's future learning and social interactions.

In addition to classroom resources, Think Equal will offer workshops for parents and caregivers, encouraging a collaborative approach to children's emotional education. This aspect of the programme aims to ensure that the lessons learned in school are reinforced at home, creating a consistent learning environment.

The launch of Think Equal is part of a broader effort to improve educational outcomes in Rochdale. Local authorities and educational bodies are increasingly recognising the role of emotional intelligence in academic success and overall well-being.

As the programme rolls out, schools in Rochdale will begin implementing the Think Equal curriculum in the upcoming academic year. This initiative is expected to have a positive impact on children's social skills and emotional health, contributing to a more supportive educational environment.

articles.title: Calls for Change in Rochdale Rape Law by Former Attorneys General
articles.excerpt: Former attorneys general have urged for changes to the law regarding rape cases in Rochdale, highlighting the need for reform to better support victims and ensure justice.
articles.content_html:
Former attorneys general have called for changes to the law concerning rape cases in Rochdale. Their comments come amid ongoing discussions about the effectiveness of current legislation in addressing the needs of victims and ensuring justice is served.

The former officials expressed concern that existing laws may not adequately protect victims or provide them with the support they require during legal proceedings. They highlighted the importance of reforming the legal framework to create a more victim-centred approach in handling such sensitive cases.

In their statements, the former attorneys general noted that many victims feel discouraged from coming forward due to the complexities and challenges associated with the legal process. They argued that changes are necessary to encourage more individuals to report incidents of sexual violence and to ensure that those who do are treated with the respect and dignity they deserve.

The call for reform has sparked discussions within the legal community and among advocacy groups, who are also pushing for changes to improve the handling of sexual offences. They believe that a review of the current laws could lead to better outcomes for victims and a more effective justice system overall.

Local residents and community leaders have been urged to engage in this conversation, as the implications of any legal changes could have a significant impact on the way such cases are prosecuted in Rochdale. The former attorneys general emphasised the need for a collaborative approach to ensure that any reforms are practical and beneficial to all parties involved.

As discussions continue, it remains to be seen what specific changes may be proposed and how they will be implemented. Stakeholders are encouraged to remain informed and involved in the ongoing dialogue surrounding this important issue.

articles.title: New Learning Mentor Role Announced for Rochdale's Alternative Provision
articles.excerpt: A new position for a Learning Mentor has been created within Rochdale's Alternative Provision, aimed at supporting students with diverse needs.
articles.content_html:
A new position for a Learning Mentor has been announced within Rochdale's Alternative Provision. This role is designed to provide support to students who require additional assistance in their educational journey.

The Learning Mentor will work closely with students, helping them to overcome barriers to learning and achieve their academic goals. This initiative reflects a commitment to inclusivity and tailored educational support within the community.

The role involves collaborating with teachers and other educational staff to create a supportive environment that fosters personal and academic growth. The Learning Mentor will also engage with families to ensure a holistic approach to each student's development.

This announcement comes as part of ongoing efforts to enhance educational provisions in Rochdale, particularly for those students who may struggle in traditional learning settings. The focus is on providing a nurturing and adaptable learning experience.

Local education officials have highlighted the importance of such roles in addressing the diverse needs of students. By employing a Learning Mentor, Rochdale aims to improve educational outcomes and support the well-being of its students.

Anyone interested in applying for the Learning Mentor position can find more information on the Diversity Dashboard website. The role is expected to contribute positively to the educational landscape in Rochdale, ensuring that all students have the opportunity to succeed.
